rationale: The query's core transferable method is the analysis of Turing instability in activator-inhibitor systems on large random networks, leading to node differentiation via a mode-indexed dispersion relation and mean-field approximation. The candidate studies biased random walks on complex networks, focusing on stationary occupation probabilities and transit times—entirely different machinery (stochastic processes vs. reaction-diffusion dynamics). However, its object (communication networks/transport processes) is genuinely different from the query's biological/chemical pattern formation, so object_difference is true.
